Wake-Up Words: “Alexa, when is my water bill due?”
Unnamed: 9: “Alexa, is my gas bill higher than last month?”
Description: Easily keep up with your utility bills with Alexa. Get an alert when you’re behind your normal payment date, your bill is higher than normal or your payment has problems clearing. Alexa answers questions about your utility bills and lets you securely pay your utility bill with just your voice.To get started just say, “Alexa, when’s my electric bill due?” or “Alexa, pay my water bill”. •  For the first time, you will need to connect your utility account to Alexa. When prompted, provide Alexa with your utility provider’s name.•  Alexa will attempt to look up your utility account using the name, billing address, and email stored in your Amazon account. Alexa will ask you for permission to do this. If you do not wish to have Alexa look up your account based on this information, she will alternately ask for permission to look up your account via the utility account number.•  Once your account is successfully connected, Alexa will tell you the upcoming due date of your electric bill. Alexa will also be enabled to deliver notifications for your electric bill.Other things to try:"Alexa, how much is my electric bill?" “Alexa, is my gas bill higher than last month?”"Alexa, when's my water bill due?"Frequent Asked Questions:What is a bill notification?When a yellow ring or an on-screen banner appears on your Alexa device, listen to the notifications by saying saying, “Alexa, read my notifications.” Notifications are meant to help you manage your bills but not overwhelm you. You’ll receive a due date notification only when the bill hasn’t been paid yet, not every time the bill is due. Alexa will give notifications only when asked.How do I connect my utility account?You can easily connect your utility account with Alexa. To set up each biller separately, just say “Alexa, connect my <electric/ water/ gas> bill.” By linking your gas, water, and electricity accounts to Alexa from your Alexa-enabled device, you can receive an alert when it looks like your payment will be late or your account has payment issues. You can also pay your utility bills with Alexa. How do I manage Alexa bill notifications?You can turn bill notifications on or off anytime in the Alexa App by tapping on the menu icon in the upper left and going to Settings, Notifications, Bill Planner, and then switching Notifications to ON or OFF.How do I stop using Alexa for my bill?Just say, “Alexa, disconnect my bill.” If you unlink an account, Alexa will stop giving you information about your bills and notifications about due dates and payments.

Description: ARIS for Alexa® Demo is developed to help you get easy access to all the information stored in ARIS. Use this skill to see how Alexa can interact directly with ARIS to get you the information you need - even if you don’t have access to your ARIS portal in that moment. The skill can answer questions about your ARIS processes and offers new ways of interaction with ARIS as an Enterprise Digital Twin by using your natural language to request information from the ARIS repository. It is valuable to get a voice output but in some cases, it is also important to get a visual feedback in parallel. For example, you can ask Alexa for an overview of the change requests that are related to your processes and you get the respective dashboard in ARIS. If you are interested in the production distribution you’ll receive additional information via Process Mining.With ARIS for Alexa®, we offer a comprehensive demo system so that you don’t need to connect it to your own database. You can ask Alexa numerous questions about ARIS and you will receive output based on our demo data. This gives you an impression on how it looks and feels like.To activate this demo skill, please follow these easy steps: 1. Activate the skill in the Alexa Skill store.2. Open the skill for the first time by initially calling it “Alexa, ask ARIS for the registration code”. Alexa will then tell you a personalized code.3. Go to https://tinyurl.com/aris-alexa and register for free for the ARIS for Alexa® Demo. Enter your personal data and the code to connect to your Alexa device. 4. After successful registration, you will receive a confirmation email from Software AG with your individual link to the visual results from skill.5. Start asking questions “Alexa, ask ARIS“ and enjoy! Do you need some inspiration? Here are some example questions:• How is the production distribution?• What is the average process cycle time?• Which process step took the longest?• How often did it occur?• Who is the CTO of United Motor Group?• What are the processes for role “Accountant”?• How is the follower distribution?• How is the conversation distribution?• What is ARIS?• What are the advantages of ARIS?

Description: Ask My Buddy helps you ask for assistance using only your voice - and - makes it easy to let your contacts know you are doing ok.The most valuable alert method is the one that is accessible when you need it! Just say Alexa, Ask My Buddy to Send Help. PREMIUM features are available via in skill purchase.  Please ensure voice purchasing is enabled.Alert feature You can't predict when you'll need help, Ask My Buddy lets you immediately alert someone in your Personal Alert Network that you need them to check on you. You may alert just 1 or all of your contacts with just one command. Simply say, 'Alexa, Ask My Buddy to alert YourContact' or 'Alexa, Ask My Buddy to send help', and Ask My Buddy will immediately send an alert, notifying them to check on you right away. Alerts will be sent by text message, an email, and a voice telephone call. Premium users can let Contacts know what type of help they may need just by saying a few more words after the Send Help command.  Contacts will receive the added information in each text message, email, and voice call.  ‘Alexa, Ask My Buddy to Send Help I have fallen and am on the floor’Check In feature Have you wished that you could easily let your family that you are doing ok? Ease their minds with a 'Check In' message to your contact by saying "Alexa, Ask My Buddy to check in with yourContact" or you can check in with all of your Contacts at once. Check In messages are sent the same way alerts are, by text message, an email, and a voice telephone call. Use Check In messages each day to let your family know you are doing OK, or use the Check In after you return from taking a walk, even kids can use it to let parents know they arrived home from school. Ask My Buddy is not a substitute for 911, but rather an additional tool offering the security of knowing help is just a shout away.If you need assistance with your account, please send email to support@askmybuddy.net (add our email to your contact list so our answer isn't marked as spam)  For hands on demonstrations and how-to videos, visit us at https://www.youtube.com/AskMyBuddy  Connect with us on Facebook at https://facebook.com/askmybuddy.net or on Twitter at https://twitter.com/askmybuddy_net Please ask your family, friends, a caregiver, or a neighbor to be part of your network, and make a plan with them for what it means if you send them an alert or a check in message. If you live in a community or building with private security, consider talking to them about being part of your network. Download our 'Guide for Contacts' at the bottom of the www.AskMyBuddy.net Settings page and share with those on the contact list. To get started, there are 2 parts to setting up Ask My Buddy. 1) Set up your account at the AskMyBuddy.net website.2) Enable and Link Accounts, in the Alexa companion application. First create your accountGo to www.AskMyBuddy.netClick on Register menu item (if you are on a mobile device, tap on the 3 lines in the upper left corner).Enter your information and press the Register buttonThen on the login page, enter your email and password and press Log-In button.Tap on the Contacts page to add your friends or family.Second Enable Ask My Buddy:Open the Alexa application and locate Ask My Buddy.Click on Enable Skill buttonThis will open our Login page. Enter the email and password used to login to  AskMyBuddy.netPress Log-In button to complete linkingOnce both steps are complete you are ready to use Ask My Buddy.Talk to your Alexa device and say one of the following phrases:To alert all of your contacts at once say:Alexa Ask My Buddy to Alert EveryoneAlexa Ask My Buddy to Send HelpTo alert only one contact say:Alexa Ask My Buddy to Alert ContactFirstNameTo use the Check In feature say:Alexa Ask My Buddy to Check In with EveryoneAlexa Ask My Buddy to tell everyone I'm OKTo Check-in with only one contact say:Alexa Ask My Buddy to Check In with ContactFirstName

Skill Name: Phone Link - Texting, Calling, Phone Finder, Music
Wake-Up Words: “Alexa, ask Phone Link to find my phone.”
Unnamed: 9: “Alexa, ask Phone Link to text Jane Doe.”
Unnamed: 10: “Alexa, ask Phone Link to play my Classic Rock playlist.”
Description:
Phone Link connects Alexa to your Android phone or tablet, giving you the ability to read out and send text messages, make phone calls, locate a lost device, and play music and audio files.

Enable the skill here then install the "Phone Link for Alexa" app from Google Play. Direct Link: phonelinkskill.com/download

ADVANCED PHONE AND TABLET LOCATOR: 

If your device is close by, you can make it ring - even if it's in silent mode or has the do not disturb setting turned on. If the device is further away, you can get its approximate address. Just say something like "Ask Phone Link to ring my phone" or "Ask Phone Link to find my tablet".

PHONE CALLS: 

You can ask Alexa to start a phone call on your phone. It will even turn on your phone's speakerphone if you don't have a Bluetooth connection. Just say something like "Ask phone link to call John Smith".

TEXT MESSAGING: 

Phone link can read out and send text messages from many popular apps, including Facebook Messenger, WhatsApp, your phone's SMS app, and many others. It is also able to read and reply to messages received in Gmail. It works by monitoring your notifications for incoming messages. This is the same system used by Wear OS and Android Auto. Just say something like "Ask Phone Link to send a text to Jane Doe." or "Ask Phone Link to read my messages."

If supported by your Alexa device, you can optionally have Alexa notify you when you have unread messages. When you see the notification light, just say "Alexa, what did I miss?"

MUSIC AND AUDIO STREAMING:

Stream music and audio files stored on your Android device to your Alexa device or app, giving Alexa access to your entire mp3 collection. You can direct Alexa to play by playlist, title, album, artist, or genre. The Phone Link app can be used to set up your playlists.

To start playback, just say something like "Ask Phone Link to play my workout playlist" or "Ask Phone Link to play some rock music".

WORKS WITH ECHO AUTO AND IN-CAR SYSTEMS:

Phone Link works with the Echo Auto and in-car systems, providing a safe way to send and receive text messages hands-free.

SUPPORTS FAMILIES AND PARENTAL MONITORING:

You can use phone link with multiple devices in your household. Just include the device's name in your voice command (e.g, "Ask Phone Link to find John's Phone").  Parents can use it to monitor the location of their children as well as the text messages they are receiving. 

GETTING STARTED:

Begin by enabling the skill here, then visit Google Play and install the "Phone Link for Alexa" app.  Run the app and follow the on-screen instructions.

Direct Download Link: https://phonelinkskill.com/download

PRIVACY:

Your privacy is very important. Text messages and contact information are stored on your phone and nowhere else. Even when you ask Alexa to read or send a message, the message is routed between your phone and Alexa device without any storage occurring on our servers.

Description: Keeping up with your family or your business assets, just got a whole lot easier thanks to GPSOnIt's My Locator skill and Alexa!  With the My Locator skill, you can just ask Alexa where your family members are. The My Locator skill connects Alexa to your GPSOnIt.com account, for easy voice access.  To hear a sample without linking your account, just enable My Locator and say, 'Alexa, ask My Locator for an example'.To use this skill, you need to have a GPSOnIt.com account.  If you do not have an account, please visit http://www.GPSOnIt.com and visit our store to find the perfect device for your needs or email us support@GPSOnIt.comGPSOnIt is a service providing real time GPS based location tracking for everything you care about.  There are many options from in-vehicle devices, wearable devices, worldwide pocket satellite devices, to tracking your own smart-phone.  No matter which type you choose, or if you choose multiple types, you can simply ask Alexa ask My Locator where everyone is.  Tracking isn't just for companies with lots of vehicles.  Families have many tracking needs.  Multiple cars, motorcycles / scooters, jet skis, perhaps a boat or RV, off highway vehicles, kayaks, and most importantly, family members. Some family members may have functional needs, such as those in the Autism Spectrum or suffering from Alzheimer's disease, and may wander, or become lost and disoriented, even while visiting familiar places.  ** With GPSOnIt, you can have peace of mind with one system. **Locator skill features include:    Real time position information for one or all devices just by asking Alexa    Real addresses    Low Battery status    Direction and speed information     In / Out / Nearest Zone (geo-fence) information    All location information automatically added to a Home card in your Alexa application and can be sent as a text message if set in your GPSOnIt.com account settings.The My Locator skill can also give you the distance and travel time for the device to reach a preset zone.just say:  'Alexa, ask My Locator how far Toyota is from work?' or 'Alexa, ask My Locator how long it will take Toyota to get Home?'Your GPSOnIt.com account includes all of our professional industry leading features, including live tracking,  Zones (geo-fences), multiple reports, satellite or typical maps, device health, and much more.Go to http://www.GPSOnIt.com and click on StoreLook for our Family / Personal tracker, or select one or more of any of our other great devices.Each Family / Personal tracker device purchased comes with 12 months of service.After your order is processed, you will receive an email confirming shipping, account details,  and instructions on how to enable and link the My Locator skill in the Alexa application.If you Love it, put a GPSOnIt, if you want to find it, ask My Locator.My Locator may not be suitable for all ages.GPSOnIt.com is a service created and operated by Whiteboard Technologies, LLC.We know how important your privacy is. We do not sell your personal information. We protect your data using SSL (Secure Socket Layers) and the latest encryption techniques.  Whiteboard Technologies, LLC provides solutions to help manage and track all of your assets — from family cars to heavy equipment — from any web enabled device. Attention GPS Tracking Companies, If you would like to voice enable your service please contact us.

Description: * Keep connected to your Lincoln Connect vehicle with the Lincoln Way Alexa skill. Lincoln Connect is an optional feature on all 2018 vehicles (excluding the MKT) and on all future vehicles. An embedded modem is an optional feature on select 2015 – 2020 vehicles. The Lincoln Way Alexa skill works with Lincoln Connect compatible vehicles only.Lincoln Way users with Lincoln Connect compatible vehicles can use the Alexa Voice Service to issue a variety of remote commands and obtain vehicle information. The Lincoln Way skill allows you to give commands, such as:Start Engine“Alexa, ask Lincoln Way to start my car with PIN 1234.”Stop Engine“Alexa, ask Lincoln Way to stop the engine.”Lock Vehicle“Alexa, ask Lincoln Way to lock my vehicle.”Unlock Vehicle“Alexa, ask Lincoln Way to unlock my car.”Last Command Status (used after lock/unlock and start/stop commands)“Alexa, ask Lincoln Way what is the status of my last command?”Vehicle Mileage“Alexa, ask Lincoln Way for the odometer reading.”Vehicle Range“Alexa, ask Lincoln Way how much gas do I have?”Tire Pressure “Alexa, ask Lincoln Way is my tire pressure low?”Selected Vehicle“Alexa, ask Lincoln Way what is my primary vehicle?”List Vehicles“Alexa, ask Lincoln Way to list all cars on the account.”Switch Selected Vehicle“Alexa, ask Lincoln Way to switch my primary vehicle.”Vehicle Charge Level (only available for compatible electric vehicles)"Alexa, ask Lincoln Way for my vehicle's charge level."Vehicle Charge Status (only available for compatible electric vehicles)"Alexa, ask Lincoln Way to tell me my vehicle's charge status."To use this skill:· Enable the Lincoln Way skill· Log in to your Lincoln Way account within the Alexa mobile app using your Lincoln Way username and password· Set a PIN to help protect access to certain features when using the skill· Select your vehicle that you want to interact with· To get help at any time, just say “Alexa, ask Lincoln Way for help.”For support with the skill, call Lincoln Concierge through the Lincoln Way mobile app.Disclaimers:* For more information on Lincoln Way, visit www.lincoln.com. ** Lincoln Connect requires a compatible cellular network. This skill interfaces with the Granville Public Library.  With it, you can do the following: *  Search for items by saying "lookup," and then the title of the item.*  Check if Granville Public Library has an item by saying "Does Granville Library have" and then the title of the item.*  Get a days events from the calendar by saying "what is happening today?" or "what is happening on October 31st?"*  Retrieve the libraries hours for a given day by saying "what are the hours for today?" or "what are the hours on October 31st?"If you are a patron with the Granville Public Library, you can login by reading off your complete library card number at any time.  Then, you will be prompted to enter your pin number.  Afterwards, you will be able to do the following:*  Submit holds by saying "reserve," and then the title of the item you wish to reserve.  When there are multiple items with similar titles, it may help to specify the form you are looking for.  For example, "Reserve title book," or, "reserve title DVD."*  Retrieve your holds by saying "what holds do I have?".  You can also limit this to holds that are ready for pickup by saying "What holds are ready for pickup?"*  Cancel a hold by saying "cancel my hold on" and then the title.  Note: you cannot cancel a hold once it is ready for pickup. *  Renew an item by saying "renew" and then the title.*  Retrieve a list of all items you have out by saying "What items do I have out?"  	 Retrieve a list of overdue items by saying "What overdue items do I have?"	 Retrieve a list of 'lost' items by saying "What lost items do I have?"*  Check what fines/fees you have by saying "What fines do I have?" If you are having trouble with the skill, you can say "help" for some simple assistance.To get a complete list of commands, say "list commands."

Description: I find myself asking Alexa what's for dinner all the time. She always has a stock response, but it's not really an actionable answer, unless I want to order pizza every night. (Shout out to the Domino's skill.) And recipe skills are too complicated over voice--the search functionality and deliverance method just isn't optimized for Alexa yet. I needed a skill that would just get me started. "Yellow curry chicken with cauliflower rice." "Ground beef in spicy arrabiata sauce, mixed with heavy cream and mozzerella cheese." Sounds great, Alexa, thanks for reminding me. There's about 30 ideas, and I'll be happy to add any suggestions! This is only my second skill, so it's a simple one-time interaction model right now. It tells you the recipe AND it sends it to your app/Show! I'm hoping to add a level soon that allows you to ask her for just beef recipes, or just easy recipes, or just sides. For some, these recipe ideas might be too obvious--and for some, they might be too new! I'm aiming for a good balance, and adding new things all the time. Thank you for trying it out!

Are you looking to explore the world of avocado oil?  The Founders of Bella Vado California Avocado Oil are excited to share the many uses of avocado oil and its healthy benefits.Bella Vado California Avocado Oil presents the Bella Vado Alexa Skill!  Once you enable the skill:  - Listen to Daily Tips about the uses of Avocado Oil- Ask A Question Listen to the Daily Tip of the day!· Learn how to make avocado soup· How does avocado oil taste?· Learn how avocado oil can moisturize· Are avocados fruits or veggies?· Learn about avocado nutrients - How is avocado oil processed?Sample questions to ask Bella Vado:· Is Bella Vado avocado oil free from parabens?· Is avocado oil healthy?· How is avocado oil processed?· Is Bella Vado avocado oil cold pressed?· Who is Bella Vado California Avocado Oil?· What are the health benefits of avocado oil?· What is the shelf life of Bella Vado avocado oil?· Is there butter flavored avocado oil?· Does Bella Vado have avocado face scrubs?· How is avocado oil extracted?· Does avocado oil have a high temperature cooking point?· Is Bella Vado avocado oil organic?Disclaimer: The health benefits of avocado oil in this skill is provided for informational and educational purposes only. Please call your doctor to receive medical advice.ABOUT BELLA VADO CALIFORNIA AVOCADO OIL:Bella Vado is a family owned business located on their 32 acre, Organic, chemical free grove and processing facility in southern California. Bella Vado Avocado Oil co-founder, Corinne da Silva says, when we look back, we realize how quickly it all flew by. Late night bottling parties calling in all of our kids and their friends who were well bribed with pizza and music. The stressful seventy two hour oil pressing runs where we rotated sleeping shifts. We did this while half of us worked full time jobs! We were dedicated. We hustled. We were heads down. We loved the art of the process of pressing our avocados into avocado oil. We spent time perfecting the process and looking to constantly improve our own oil each year. To us, Bella Vado is the heart of the avocado!CERTIFICATIONS AND AWARDS:Bella Vado avocado oil has been selected by the USDA to participate in setting CODEX standards world-wide for Extra Virgin Avocado Oil.  It is our expertise and care that positioned the company for this valuable and honored position.- Good Food Awards- USDA Certified Organic- Non GMO Certified- San Diego Grown 365• To learn more about Bella Vado California Avocado Oil:  BellaVado.com• Support Email: bellavado.com/pages/contact-us

Description: Headspace is your guide to health and happiness. With the Headspace Alexa skill, you can build a daily meditation practice, create the conditions for a better night’s sleep, or go on guided walks and runs. HOW DOES IT WORK?Just ask your Alexa skill to talk to Headspace. We'll have daily meditations, guided walks and runs when you need some fresh air, and sleep exercises for when you're ready to wind down. You can say…- Alexa, open Headspace- Alexa, tell Headspace I'm ready to meditate- Alexa, ask Headspace for today's meditation- Alexa, tell Headspace I want to wind down- Alexa, ask Headspace to help me fall asleep- Alexa, tell Headspace to start a mindful walk- Alexa, ask Headspace for a running meditation HOW DO I CONNECT TO MY HEADSPACE ACCOUNT?To connect to your Headspace account, just say "connect my account" on the Alexa skill. A prompt will appear, asking you to sign in. You’ll have access to daily content as well as our Basics pack. This skill is intended for people who decide in the morning that they will be taking a mental health day for their depression. Once the skill is activated, it will guide users through their morning, reminding them to accomplish regular, daily tasks, as well as complete other tasks that are intended to boost their mood. By the end of the task, the user will be left with some activities to be completed later on in the day for the purpose of not having a completely unplanned day. The goal of this skill is for the user to be feeling better by the end of the day so that they can resume normal responsibilities by the following morning. By creating the voice of a Mental Health Manager that balances a somewhat authoritative tone with comedic relief, the user will be prompted with a morning schedule while being occasionally met with unexpected humor. This voice reminds users that taking a mental health day can be very useful and, even at times, fun. This skill is not intended by any means to cure depression, and is also not a guarantee to help users feel better or less depressed. This skill was also not created by licensed psychotherapists, psychiatrists, or doctors of any kind. If at any point while using the skill the user starts to feel suicidal, 911 should be called immediately and this cannot be done through this Alexa skill.This skill was built with love by VoiceApps.com. Esta vibración en 396 Hz estimula las áreas principales que actúan en tu cerebro cuando sientes placer. Deja sonar esta frecuencia de fondo y hará lo suyo. Casi sin darte cuenta, se irán aplacando las zonas del miedo y la culpa, dándole rienda suelta a tu cerebro para interpretar el placer puro. Y no hace falta que el volumen esté fuerte. Alcanza y sobra con que suene de fondo.Funciona para una o más personas. Si quieres acelerar el proceso, recuéstate o siéntate en un lugar cómodo, cierra los ojos y respira profundamente, inhalando por la nariz y exhalando por la boca. Lentamente, la Frecuencia del Placer hará que tu mente se centre en las sensaciones del cuerpo. Cada parte que estimules enviará un mensaje amplificado al cerebro. Al cabo de unos 20 minutos ingresarás a un nuevo espacio sensorial. ¡Que lo disfrutes!

Unnamed: 10: “water flower number two for five times”
Description: A game designed for kids to manage flowers. Please see the description to know how the game works.  Start your game by saying "open my garden", system will create a new game for you if you don't have previous game record or load your previous game. After entering the game, say "report" to get the flowers' initial information. You will start your game with one flower in seed stage and you will have maximum of 3 flowers to manage later. After your caring, the flower will grow to "germination", "growth", "spreading" stages. Try to keep the water amount  and the fertilizer amount as much as you can by saying something like "water flower number one for five times" and "fertilize flower number one for three times", but no more than 100%, which makes the flower drowned and poisoned. flower will be dried out if the water amount drop to 0%. Each action makes 10% difference in the original amount. Yes! the water amount and fertilizer amount is designed to decrease in real time! Check your flowers the second day you play and get a sense of the decreasing speed.The reproduction will happen in spreading stage and the fertilizer amount will influence the reproduction rate of the flower. The more you keep it to the maximum the more possible it will generate the seeds (check back frequently to see if seeds are generated in this stage!) It takes about 11 days to reach spreading stage.If too much water and fertilizer are applied to flowers, try saying "pour out/dig our water/fertilizer on flower number one". say "give me a hint" for more secrete game information!say "stop" to save and quit. Only deliberately quitting your game will make game saved!say "check my score" to see your score.If you can't play the game for a period of time, you can say "pause" to prevent water and fertilizer dropping till the next time you open the gameDuring some period of the game stage, your flowers may attract birds to drop seeds in the garden!

You can also get information on active weather alerts / warnings in your area to stay safe, and find out when it might start raining near you. Now supporting daily forecast videos on your Echo Spot and Echo Show devices. Try saying things like “Alexa, ask The Weather Network how’s the weather tomorrow” or “this weekend”.  OR “Alexa, ask The Weather Network will it rain today in Vancouver”OR“Alexa, ask The Weather Network, are there any weather alerts in Burnaby.”  KEY FEATURES:Detailed WEATHER FORECASTS including current observations and temperatures, short term and long term forecasts that are updated every 15 minutes to ensure accuracy and reliability Severe weather ALERTS / WARNINGS which provide you with information on any active weather heading your way 3-DAY SEVERE WEATHER OUTLOOK which is included in responses when significant weather is expected in the next few days PRECIPITATION START & STOP times included for North America when available (for the next 3 hours) HYPER-LOCAL weather which provides you with the most accurate forecast for your location Daily weather FORECAST VIDEOS for your Canadian locations presented by our TWN on-air personalities and weather experts TIPS:Immerse yourself in weather by trying some of these questions: "Alexa, ask The Weather Network..." ...what is the weather like today?...will it rain today? ...what's the weather on Tuesday?...what’s the weather this weekend?...are there any active weather alerts?...what is the forecast for this week?...what is the forecast for next week for Miami?…to show me a forecast video. We’d love to hear from you! Please send feedback to alexa@pelmorex.com. The Car Shopper app is intended to act like a virtual salesman. As Car Shopper shows you cars you can respond by saying that you like or dislike a car. As your history grows Car Shopper will analyze your like and dislike history to show you cars that you are more likely to like. Car Shopper analyzes your like and dislike history to better suggest cars that a user may want to buy. This app also allows you to train the initial engine with a set of base preferences to further streamline the results. The only required base preference is a city. This lets our app know where to being searching for cars. All results will be in a 50 mile radius. Once the city is set Car Shopper will start showing you cars for sale. If you know some basic details such as the body style or minimum mileage you want you can set those. You can even set makes, years, and max price. With base preferences set Car Shopper starts searching. Dislikes and likes will be run through a preference algorithm to determine your interests. That information is used to pull out the result of subsequent searches that you are more inclined to like. No hard filters required. Car Shopper will find the perfect car for you based on your saved history.

Unnamed: 9: “Alexa, ask Trash Day, is it trash day?”
Description: Updated: 2017-02-22 with biweekly and monthly pickups.I can never seem to remember.  Is my trash pickup is Monday/Thursday or is it Tuesday/Friday?  And the recycling and lawn waste pickups happen on different days.  And some days I'm not sure if today is Tuesday or Wednesday anyway.  Alexa, help me!Use Trash Day to teach Alexa your regular pickup schedule.  Here is mine:  "Alexa, open Trash Day."  "Set time zone to Eastern."  "Add weekly trash pickup on Tuesday at six a.m."  "Add weekly trash pickup on Friday at six a.m."  "Add biweekly recycling pickup on this Wednesday at one a.m."  "Add biweekly lawn waste pickup on Wednesday after next at one p.m."  "Add monthly scrap metal pickup on the fifteenth at four p.m."  "Stop"Then, whenever you aren't sure if the trash needs to go out to the curb:  "Alexa, open Trash Day."  "Alexa, ask Trash Day, is it trash day?"  "Alexa, tell Trash Day, to tell me the schedule."For more extensive help, refer to the documentation at: https://s3.amazonaws.com/trash-day-docs/Help.html

Description: Want to check your camera feed without being anchored to your smartphone? No Problem! You can effortlessly monitor your Blink video security system using voice command with the Blink SmartHome Skill.To view activity from your Blink camera, say:“Alexa, show me [camera name]”Use this command to start streaming a camera live from your supported screened Alexa device.Simply say, “Alexa, hide [camera name]”, to stop and return to your supported screened Alexa device home screen:To see your most recent video clip from Blink on your supported screened Alexa device, say:“Alexa, show me the last activity from the [camera name]” To arm/disarm your Blink system, say:“Alexa, ask Blink to arm [system name]”"Alexa, ask Blink to disarm [system name], my pin is ####"To learn when the latest motion clip was recorded, say:“Alexa, ask Blink when was my last motion alert?”To learn how many unread and read motion clips you have, you can say:“Alexa, ask Blink how many motion clips do I have?”To check your scheduled arming/disarming, say:"Alexa, ask Blink what's my schedule?" To check your current Blink system status, say:“Alexa, ask Blink what is my status?"Motion Announcements is an audible notification feature for select paired Echo devices triggered by your supported Blink cameras (XT2 and Mini). As soon as motion event is triggered, Alexa will announce "Motion detected at [camera name]." A quick toggle enables Motion Announcements under your paired camera settings in the Alexa app. Routine Trigger allows you to set recurring actions of your choice for Alexa to automatically perform. To create a Routine trigger, tap the curtain menu and select "Routines" to customize your experience! For example, when supported Blink cameras detect motion, Alexa can turn on smart lighting inside or outside the house, deterring potential intruders!Alexa will remind you when your Blink camera batteries are running low, and can help set up smart reorders to proactively have batteries in hand when you need them.Note: In order to use Amazon Alexa with your Blink video security system, you’ll need a Blink account and a Blink camera system installed in your home. Then, getting started is easy:Simply link your Blink account to Alexa within the Alexa mobile app (Skills > Type in & search “Blink SmartHome” > Select “Blink SmartHome” > Select “Enable”.) and login to link your Blink account.For detailed instructions to integrate your Blink system with Blink Skill, visit support.blinkforhome.com and search “Alexa Setup and Troubleshooting Guide”.Blink is the fastest-growing home security and video monitoring system on the market today. Blink is powered by two AA lithium batteries and offers motion-activated HD video alerts, live streaming, scheduled arming, free cloud storage, and a built-in temperature sensor and microphone. Visit www.blinkforhome.com to learn more.

Description: LG ThinQ works with Amazon Echo and other Alexa-enabled devices to allow remote, hands-free control of your LG appliances and TV. There's no need to be in front of the appliance to check its status, simply ask Alexa to do it.'LG ThinQ - Complete' supports Washer, Dryer, Refrigerator, Air Conditioner, Robot Vacuum, Air Purifier, Oven, Dishwasher, Styler, Dehumidifier and TV (2018/2019 LG ThinQ AI TVs only) in the United States.To check device models which are compatible with Amazon Alexa, go to https://www.lg.com/us/support/smart-thinq-alexa-voice-controlThis skill will not discover new devices. As applicable, please enable the ‘LG ThinQ – Basic’ skill to discover your device.[Getting Started](Note: The account created from the www.lg.com/us website does not link with LG ThinQ app nor Alexa Set Up app from the TV. Please create a LG Account from the LG ThinQ mobile app or the Account Management app in the LG TV.)To set up your LG appliances for 'LG ThinQ - Complete' skill,1. Make sure you have registered your appliances in LG ThinQ mobile app. (Note : You can download the app in Google Play or Apple App Store.)2. Open Amazon Alexa app and enable 'LG ThinQ - Complete'' skill.3. Sign in with LG account to complete setup.To set up your LG TV for 'LG ThinQ - Complete' skill,1. For webOS 4.0, launch the "Set Up TV for Amazon Alexa" app from the Home screen on your LG TV.For webOS 4.5, launch the "Link to Devices for Voice Control" menu from Settings > Connection on your LG TV.(Note: To check operating system version, go to Settings > General > About This TV > TV Information)2. Follow the directions to log in LG account and select the name of your TV.3. When you get to the mobile setup guide page, go to the Alexa App on your phone to enable 'LG ThinQ - Complete' skill and link accounts.4. Return to your TV to complete setup.For more information about the setup guide, please visit https://www.lg.com/us/support/smart-thinq-alexa-voice-control.[Features of 'LG ThinQ - Complete' skill]Once you enable the skill, you can check the status or control the following appliances with your voice:- Washer, Dryer: Check Remaining Cycle Time, Turn On/Off- Refrigerator: Set Temperature, Ice Plus On/Off, Fresh Air Filter On, Check Temperature, Check Status (including Water Filter)- Air Conditioner: Turn On/Off, Set Temperature, Set Mode, Set Fan Speed, Check Temperature- Robot Vacuum: Check Status, Turn On/Off, Charge Battery- Air Purifier: Check Air Quality, Turn On/Off, Set Fan Speed- Oven(Only some models are available): Check Status, Stop Cooking, Set Preheat- Dishwasher: Check Status- Styler : Check Remaining Cycle Time, Turn On/Off- Dehumidifier : Check humidity, Turn On/Off, Set Fan Speed- TV: Turn Off, Control Volume, Change Channel, Switch Input, Control Video Player, Content Search & Recommendations, Launch Apps, Change Settings, 4-way Key Navigation[Examples of Voice Commands](Note: In the example sentences, you may call your product with a device type or nickname that you set in LG ThinQ App. For the TV, you can give the nickname to your TV in the “Set Up TV for Amazon Alexa” app(webOS 4.0) or the “Link to Devices for Voice Control“ connection settings menu(webOS 4.5) on your TV.)For example, try the following after you say "Alexa, ask LG":“check the status of the washer” checks the remaining wash cycle time.“set the air conditioner to 65 degrees” sets the A/C to a specific temperature.“preheat the oven to 350 degrees” sets the Oven to preheat."volume up/down on TV" adjusts the volume level."change channel to fifteen on TV" changes the channel to the number you requested.To hear more examples, say "Alexa, ask LG to help."For more examples for the TVs, go to the “Set Up TV for Amazon Alexa” app(webOS 4.0) or the “Link to Devices for Voice Control“ connection settings menu(webOS 4.5) on your TV.https://www.lg.com/us/support/email-appointment

Unnamed: 10: “Alexa, ask SHIELD to find my remote”
Description: NVIDIA SHIELD TV is a powerful 4K HDR streaming media player, GeForce® gaming machine, and smart home hub. With the Alexa skill, you can now use an Amazon Echo device to control your SHIELD TV using just your voice.GETTING STARTED1. In your SHIELD TV settings, add a SHIELD account (Settings > Accounts > Add Account > SHIELD).  To log in, use your existing NVIDIA credentials or create a new account.  Note: This account is different from the Google account used to log in to SHIELD.2. Make sure your SHIELD TV is up to date with the latest software (Settings > About > Check for upgrades).3. On the Google Play Store app on your SHIELD TV, make sure the “SHIELD TV – Alexa skill” app is up to date (Google Play Store app > My apps > update all apps)4. Select the “Enable” button to complete the setup and link your SHIELD TV.Note: You will be asked to log in using the same NVIDIA credentials from Step 1.When talking to Alexa, refer to your SHIELD TV by the device name. You can view or change the device name by going to Settings > About > Device name on your SHIELD TV. The default name is “SHIELD”.Note: If you are changing the name of a device that has already been paired, you may need to run device discovery on Alexa to update the name in your Alexa App.THINGS TO TRY:Turn on/off:“Alexa, turn on [device name].”“Alexa, turn off [device name].”Open apps: ¹“Alexa, open Netflix on [device name].""Alexa, open Prime Video."Control volume ¹  ²“Alexa, turn up volume on [device name].” “Alexa, set volume to 80 percent.”“Alexa, mute.”Control media playback ¹“Alexa, pause on [device name].” “Alexa, play.”“Alexa, next.”“Alexa, previous.”“Alexa, fast-forward ten minutes.” ³“Alexa, rewind one minute.” ³“Alexa, start over on [device name].” ³Navigate SHIELD ¹“Alexa, go to settings on [device name].” “Alexa, go home on [device name].”“Alexa, go to control settings.” - Brings up controller settings to see battery levelsFind remote commands"Alexa, ask SHIELD to find my remote.""Alexa, ask SHIELD to find my SHIELD remote.""Alexa, ask SHIELD where my remote is.""Alexa, ask SHIELD where my SHIELD remote is.""Alexa, ask SHIELD to locate my remote.""Alexa, ask SHIELD to locate my SHIELD remote."Note: Use the term SHIELD when accessing remote commands.¹ You may need to say “on [device name]” for the first command when talking to Alexa if you have multiple devices. For follow-on commands, you won’t need to say “on [device name]”.² If volume control is set to “IR” on your SHIELD TV, Alexa cannot control the volume level.³ Advanced media playback controls (FF/RW) currently work in HBO, Google Play Movies & TV, Hulu, and Vudu.

Description: Voice control for your Nox just got more intuitive. Alexa now includes light, Sleep-Aid, alarm control. You can also ask Alexa temperature indoors and your sleep quality. Unnamed: 10: “Alexa, ask Bose to play this song on all of my speakers.”
Description: If you own a Bose SoundTouch speaker, the Bose Skill enables you to control elements of your streaming music experience by voice. With the Skill, you can talk to Alexa to play your SoundTouch presets, pause and resume the music, skip tracks, adjust volume, find out what’s playing, or turn your speaker off.  And if you have more than one SoundTouch speaker, just say the word and you can play the same music on all of them.   Here are a few useful things to try: "Alexa, ask Bose to play Preset 1"“Alexa, ask Bose to skip this song”“Alexa, ask Bose to turn up the volume”"Alexa, ask Bose what's playing”“Alexa, ask Bose to pause” “Alexa, ask Bose to resume”“Alexa, ask Bose to turn off”If you have multiple SoundTouch speakers, you can say “Alexa, ask Bose to play this song everywhere” to play the same music on all of your speakers.  When playing on all of your speakers, most voice requests – such as “Alexa, ask Bose to skip this song” or “Alexa, ask Bose to turn off” – will control the entire group.  You can also control specific speakers by using their names.  For example, "Alexa, ask Bose to play Preset 1 in the bedroom", “Alexa, ask Bose to skip the song in the kitchen”, “Alexa, ask Bose to turn up the volume in the dining room”, "Alexa, ask Bose what's playing in the living room”, or “Alexa, ask Bose to turn off the patio”. Note that turning off speakers may disband the speakers group.To learn more about controlling multiple SoundTouch speakers, please see www.soundtouch.com/groupingBy enabling the Skill, any interactions you have with Bose through the Skill are subject to the Bose SoundTouch Privacy Policy provided below.  The Skill is compatible with all Bose SoundTouch speakers.  Please note that you will need to link your SoundTouch account with Alexa in order to use the Skill.  If you are a new SoundTouch owner, you will need to create your SoundTouch account first.  For more information, please see soundtouch.com

LOCATE (DEEBOT 500/700/900 Series, OZMO 600/700/900/920/930/950/960 Series only)- "Alexa, ask DEEBOT where it is?"- "Alexa, tell DEEBOT it has 10 seconds to show its face."- "Alexa, tell DEEBOT I miss it."- "Alexa, tell DEEBOT I said Marco." DOCK- "Alexa, ask DEEBOT to return to dock."- "Alexa, ask DEEBOT to return to the charging dock."- "Alexa, tell DEEBOT I said goodnight."- "Alexa, tell DEEBOT it’s time to hit the sack."- "Alexa, tell DEEBOT it’s past its bedtime."- "Alexa, ask DEEBOT to start charging with kitty." Prior set up required in the iRobot Home App.*Compatible with select Roomba® and Braava jet® robot modelsSTART• "Alexa, ask Roomba to start vacuuming"• "Alexa, ask Braava to start mopping"• "Alexa, ask Roomba to clean with *Rosie and *Bob"***STOP• "Alexa, tell Roomba to stop vacuuming"• "Alexa, tell Braava to stop sweeping"PAUSE / RESUME• "Alexa, tell Roomba to pause/resume vacuuming"• "Alexa, ask Braava to pause/resume mopping"SEND BACK TO CHARGING STATION• "Alexa, tell Roomba to go home"• "Alexa, tell Roomba to go recharge"• "Alexa, tell iRobot to return to its dock"LOCATE / STOP LOCATE• "Alexa, ask Roomba where it is"• "Alexa, ask Braava to sing for me"• "Alexa, tell Roomba I found it"• "Alexa, tell Roomba to be quiet"STATUS• "Alexa, ask Roomba what it's doing"• "Alexa, ask Braava if it's done yet"SCHEDULING• "Alexa, ask Roomba to schedule vacuuming"• "Alexa, tell Roomba to schedule cleaning on Monday at 10 AM"• "Alexa, ask Braava to schedule mopping in the morning"• "Alexa, tell Roomba to list my cleaning schedule"• "Alexa, tell Roomba to cancel vacuuming on Tuesdays"CLEANING SPECIFIC ROOMS, ZONES, OBJECTS (works with Imprint™ Smart Mapping robots; prior setup required in the iRobot HOME app)• "Alexa, ask Roomba to vacuum the master bedroom"• "Alexa, ask Braava to mop my kitchen"• "Alexa, tell Roomba there is a mess in the kitchen and living room"**• "Alexa, tell Roomba to vacuum and Braava to mop the kitchen"***• "Alexa, tell Roomba to start *Rosie in the living room and *Bob in the kitchen"***• “Alexa, tell Roomba to vacuum in front of the couch”• “Alexa, tell iRobot to clean the dinnertime favorite”• "Alexa, ask Roomba to schedule vacuuming in the dining room on Tuesdays"*Rosie and Bob are example robot names. Once you know the name of your thermostat, you can say the following: Getting the temperature:“Alexa, ask ecobee what the current temperature is.”“Alexa, ask ecobee how cold it is in the Living Room.”Getting the current set point / mode:“Alexa, ask ecobee what the Living Room is set to.”“Alexa, ask ecobee if heating is enabled.”Setting the temperature:“Alexa, tell ecobee to set the temperature in my Bedroom.”“Alexa, ask ecobee to set the Downstairs temperature to 72.” Resuming your schedule:“Alexa, tell ecobee to return to my schedule.”“Alexa, ask ecobee to cancel hold.”Increasing the temperature:“Alexa, tell ecobee I'm cold.”“Alexa, ask ecobee to make it warmer by 3 degrees.” Decreasing the temperature:“Alexa, tell ecobee I'm hot.”“Alexa, ask ecobee to make it cooler by 4 degrees.”Running the fan:“Alexa, tell ecobee to turn fan on.”“Alexa, ask ecobee to turn on fan in my Bedroom.”Setting Away, Home and Sleep comfort settings:“Alexa, tell ecobee I'm back home.”“Alexa, ask ecobee to set my thermostat to home mode.”“Alexa, tell ecobee I'm going out.”“Alexa, tell ecobee good night.” Changing mode:“Alexa, tell ecobee to turn on heat mode.”“Alexa, ask ecobee to activate cool mode.”“Alexa, tell ecobee to turn off auto mode.”Note: If switching modes, your ecobee should be switched to Off mode first. Unnamed: 9: “Alexa, ask Xbox what can I say?”
Description: With the Xbox Skill for Alexa, you can interact with your Xbox One using just your voice. Turn it on and off, launch games and apps, play and pause videos, and more, all from any Alexa-enabled device or Alexa apps on iOS and Android.To get started, sign in to the Xbox One console you want to control, tap "Enable Skill" in the Alexa app, and sign in with the Microsoft account you're using on that console. The Xbox you're signed in to will be automatically linked (you can also enable it on your console under Settings > Kinect & devices > Digital assistants). To change the device name of your Xbox or add additional consoles to control, go to the Smart Home Devices section of the Alexa app.To learn what the Xbox skill can do, just say “Alexa, ask Xbox what can I say?" Here are some commands to get you started.Standard commandsFor access to all the commands available for the Xbox skill on Alexa, use the standard command structure, like this:•	"Alexa, tell Xbox to turn on." (Make sure instant on is enabled on your console.)•	"Alexa, tell Xbox to turn off."•	"Alexa, tell Xbox to launch Forza Horizon 4" (to turn on your console, sign in, and start the game).•	"Alexa, tell Xbox to pause" (while a video is playing on Xbox).•	"Alexa, tell Xbox to resume."•	"Alexa, tell Xbox to turn it up."•	"Alexa, tell Xbox to turn volume down 5."•	"Alexa, tell Xbox to start a party."NOTE: Both "Tell Xbox" and "Ask Xbox" work for standard commands.Smart home commandsYou can also target the Xbox One console as a smart home device. Alexa smart home commands work for power, volume, and media controls. With smart home commands, you can drop the "Tell Xbox" or "Ask Xbox" portion, like in these examples:•	"Alexa, turn on [device name]."•	"Alexa, turn off [device name]."•	"Alexa, pause on [device name]."•	"Alexa, resume on [device name]."•	"Alexa, volume up on [device name]."The default device name for Xbox consoles is "Xbox". Additional consoles have unique default names like "Xbox 4B1D". You can change the device name for smart home commands to something else – "Basement Xbox" or "Den Xbox" for example. This becomes especially important if you want to use Alexa with more than one Xbox. You can change the device name for your Xbox under Smart Home Devices in the Alexa mobile app.If you've recently used your Xbox One and there aren't any other smart home devices paired with Alexa, you can drop the device name altogether, like this:•	"Alexa, pause"•	"Alexa, play"•	"Alexa, resume"•	"Alexa, skip forward"TV & video skill commandsA smaller set of video skill commands are used for TV and game functions on Xbox. You can enable and manage video skills under Settings > TV & Video > Xbox in the Alexa app. Examples include:•	"Alexa, launch Forza Horizon 4."•	"Alexa, launch Forza Horizon 4 on Xbox."•	"Alexa, launch Netflix."•	"Alexa, launch Netflix on Xbox."•	"Alexa, watch PBS."•	"Alexa, watch PBS on Xbox."NOTE: Your Alexa device needs to be linked with your console via the TV & video setting to enable these TV & video skill commands.For troubleshooting, including discovering your console and working with multiple consoles or users, see our support content and forums:•	https://aka.ms/XboxSkillSupport•	https://aka.ms/XboxRedditForum

Description: Lost your phone? Don't panic. With Find My Phone, you can easily call your lost phone from Alexa. Works with iPhone, Android, Samsung, and any other device! The skill will find your phone even if it is on silent! No need to install additional apps. All countries are supported! You can add phone numbers for all your family members. The skill can differentiate individual speakers and use different phone numbers. INSTRUCTIONSAdd phone numbers for each family member!1. To set up your phone, say “Alexa, open Find My Phone” and you’ll be asked for your phone number.Please tell your phone number in international format. The number should start with your country code. For example, US-based number: 1 415 5552671UK-based number: 44 20 7183 87502. If you want your phone to ring even in silent mode, save phone number +1 (914) 252-3987 as a contact on your phone and enable emergency bypass (see instructions below)3. You’re all set up! If you’ve lost your phone, say “Alexa, ask Find My Phone to call me”. Alexa will call your phone. Once you have found the device, simply say "Alexa, stop call" to stop ringing. Alexa will call you from:+ 1 (914) 252-3987If you want to add a phone number for another family member, please configure your Voice Profile and make sure that you have the "Personalize skills" toggle enabled in the Voice Profile Settings of the Alexa app. Then ask them to invoke the skill and add a new phone number. SUBSCRIPTIONWith Find My Phone subscription, you will get 10 calls each month. You can call mobile phones in any country. Find My Phone does not support landlines. RINGS EVEN ON SILENT!To ensure your phone will ring even in silent mode, follow the instructions depending on your device:iOS: 1. Create a new contact with phone number + 1 (914) 252-3987 2. Tap "Ringtone" in the contact editing screen    3. Check the "Emergency Bypass" option and tap Done twice.Android: 1. The Android settings vary by version and device, but you can usually get to the Do Not Disturb controls by swiping down from the top of the screen to the Quick Settings box. 2. Tap the "Do Not Disturb" icon in the drop-down menu > Allow Exceptions > Calls From > Favourite Contacts Only 3. Then open Contacts app 4. Save a new contact with phone number + 1 (914) 252-3987 and set it as a favorite by tapping the star next to the contact name.See the detailed instructions with screenshots here: voody.co/product/find-my-phonePRIVACYWe do not sell your cell phone number to third-party companies. Unnamed: 10: “Alexa, open Time Master and ask what is the time?”
Description: Simply put, the Unix timestamp is a way to track time as a running total of seconds. This count starts at the Unix Epoch on January 1st, 1970 at UTC. Therefore, the Unix timestamp is merely the number of seconds between a particular date and the Unix Epoch. It should also be pointed out that this point in time technically does not change no matter where you are located on the globe. This is very useful to computer systems for tracking and sorting dated information in dynamic and distributed applications both online and client side. The reason why Unix timestamps are used by many webmasters is because they can represent all time zones at once.

Unnamed: 9: “Repeat after me, you are the best.”
Description: Have you always wanted to get Alexa to say certain things?  With this skill you can tell Alexa to say almost anything you can think of!  You can also hear Alexa translate your phrase from English to a different language! This skill will also allow you to select between 30+ different voices and many different voice modes to repeat your phrase! You can change Alexa's accent and have her talk like a British Male!  You can also have Alexa talk fast, slow, with a high voice, or a low voice, backwards, in reverse, whispering, in a drunk voice, as a robot, spelling out words, and saying words with random beeps!  Try it out and have tons of fun!This skill is also helpful if you want to practice your language skills.  You can translate your phrase from English to a few other different languages. After translating you can hear the translation repeated slower by saying, "say it again slow" or "say it again really slow".  You can also view the translation in your Alexa app.How it works:Tell Alexa to repeat your phrase by saying, "repeat after me".  Next, you can tell her what to say.After she tells you the phrase, you can have her repeat it by saying, "say it again".You can change the way she says the phrase by saying something like, "say it again fast".To change her accent, say something like "change accent to British male", or just say "change accent" for a random accent.After you tell her a phrase, you can also tell her to translate it to another language! (Currently max of 10 translations per week)Below are some examples on how to translate a phrase, change the accent, or change the voice mode.Translating a phrase:First start by saying "repeat after me".  Then tell Alexa your phrase.After she hears and remembers the phrase, you can say something like, "translate to German".This skill allows you to translate from English to the following languages: English, French, German, Hindi, Italian, Japanese, Portuguese, and Spanish.To hear your original phrase, you can say something like, what was my original phrase?To hear the last translated phrase, say something like, what was my last translation.You can also view the translation in your Alexa app to check for accuracy.*Note: There is currently a max of 10 translations per week.  This is subject to change.*Note: Due to the nature of voice and translation technology, translations may not always be accurate.  Always check translations before using.Changing Accents:For a list of accents, say "list accents".Just say "change accent to" followed by the name of an accent.Available Accents - Alexa, American male (1, 2), American female (1, 2, 3, 4), Australian male, Australian female, British male, British female (1, 2), French male, French female (1, 2, 3), German male, German female (1, 2), Indian female (1, 2), Italian male, Italian female (1, 2), Japanese male, Japanese female, Portuguese male, Portuguese female (1, 2), Spanish male (1, 2), Spanish female (1, 2, 3, 4, 5), boy, girlChanging Voice Modes:For a list of voice modes, say "list voice modes".Just say "say it again", followed by the name of the voice mode.Available Voice Modes - fast, slow, high, low, backwards, rewind, whisper, beep, spelled out, robot, drunk, valley girl, random*Note: Whisper mode only works for certain voices.Alexa will also include her own random comments before reciting your phrase.  To turn these off, you can say "Turn off comments".You can also turn off hint prompts, which are mentioned after your phase is repeated by saying something like, "Turn hints off".

It is one of the main sources of drinking water in Israel. The sea level must stay between two red lines,  a lower and upper red lines. The sea level is measured every weekday. This skill provides information about the current sea level, the change from the day before and the distance to the two red lines.
